classification.label: regional arrival concentration
classification.theory: The excess has a strongest arrival region, but it is too spatially broad to call a single destination from the endpoint clusters alone.

classification.label: specific departure anchor
classification.theory: A continuous endpoint cluster generated a large share of excess departures; this is the data shape expected from a specific airport or airport group emptying out.

classification.label: regional departure concentration
classification.theory: The excess has a strongest departure region, but it is too spatially broad to call a single origin from the endpoint clusters alone.

classification.label: specific arrival anchor
classification.theory: A continuous endpoint cluster absorbed a large share of excess arrivals; this is the data shape expected from a specific airport or airport group drawing traffic in.

classification.label: regional departure concentration
classification.theory: The excess has a strongest departure region, but it is too spatially broad to call a single origin from the endpoint clusters alone.

classification.label: diffuse national redistribution
classification.theory: The excess is spread across many endpoint clusters; this looks more like broad return-to-work or holiday travel than one origin.

classification.label: regional departure concentration
classification.theory: The excess has a strongest departure region, but it is too spatially broad to call a single origin from the endpoint clusters alone.

classification.label: specific departure anchor
classification.theory: A continuous endpoint cluster generated a large share of excess departures; this is the data shape expected from a specific airport or airport group emptying out.

classification.label: regional arrival concentration
classification.theory: The excess has a strongest arrival region, but it is too spatially broad to call a single destination from the endpoint clusters alone.

classification.label: regional arrival concentration
classification.theory: The excess has a strongest arrival region, but it is too spatially broad to call a single destination from the endpoint clusters alone.
